release.goal: Deterministic and reproducible first release builds across backend, frontend, containers, and CI

findings.problem: Python dependencies are listed without fixed versions.
Python project metadata is not being used as a strict release lock source.
Frontend dependencies use version ranges like caret (^), so installs can drift over time.
Frontend image uses npm install instead of npm ci, reducing reproducibility.
Backend image uses python:3.11-slim instead of an exact image tag or digest.
Frontend image uses node:20-slim instead of an exact image tag or digest.
Compose uses ollama/ollama:latest, which is floating and not release-safe.
Global npm package opencode-ai is installed without a fixed version.
System packages are installed via apt-get without snapshot pinning, so package resolution may drift.
CI runtime and action pinning was not verified and must be checked before release.

tasks.title: Choose one Python release locking strategy
options: Keep pyproject.toml with semantic ranges and generate a locked release artifact such as requirements.lock or constraints-release.txt
Use exact versions directly in pyproject.toml and mirror them into requirements.txt
tasks.recommended: Keep pyproject.toml developer-friendly, but generate a dedicated locked release file and make Docker/CI install only from that file.
done_when: A single authoritative release lock file exists
Backend Docker build uses only the locked release file
CI install path uses only the locked release file

tasks.title: Pin all Python runtime dependencies
actions: Resolve all direct and transitive Python dependencies to fixed versions
Commit the lock output to the repository
Separate dev-only dependencies from release/runtime dependencies if not already separated
done_when: No release build step installs Python packages from unbounded names
Rebuilding the same commit resolves the same Python dependency graph

tasks.title: Make frontend dependency installation deterministic
actions: Remove ^ and ~ ranges from frontend-angular/package.json for release-critical dependencies
Keep package-lock.json committed and up to date
Change frontend-angular/Dockerfile from npm install to npm ci
done_when: npm ci succeeds from a clean checkout
Rebuilding the same commit resolves identical frontend packages

tasks.title: Pin container base images and service images
actions: Replace python:3.11-slim with an exact tag, ideally a digest
Replace node:20-slim with an exact tag, ideally a digest
Replace ollama/ollama:latest with an exact version or digest
Review any other service images for floating tags
done_when: No Dockerfile or Compose file uses latest
All release-relevant images use exact tags or digests

tasks.title: Pin globally installed tools
actions: Install opencode-ai with an explicit version
Document why this exact version is required
Add a smoke test proving the expected CLI version is present in the built image
done_when: Global CLI installs no longer float
Image build contains the expected tool version

tasks.title: Reduce drift from apt packages
actions: Minimize apt-installed packages to what runtime really needs
Consider a prebuilt internal base image or Debian snapshot pinning for stronger reproducibility
At minimum, document the exact base image used for release builds
done_when: Apt usage is minimized and documented
Release rebuild variance from system packages is reduced

tasks.title: Audit CI version pinning
actions: Pin setup-node and setup-python to exact runtime versions
Pin GitHub Actions to stable versions or commit SHAs where appropriate
Ensure CI uses the same lock inputs as local and Docker release builds
done_when: CI does not resolve a different dependency graph than release builds

tasks.title: Define supported release environment versions
actions: Document exact supported Python version
Document exact supported Node version
Document exact supported Docker/Compose baseline
Document expected service versions for Redis, Postgres, Ollama, and Playwright/Chromium where relevant
done_when: README or release docs clearly state supported versions
Developers can reproduce the intended build/runtime environment

tasks.title: Add release reproducibility verification
actions: Build backend and frontend images from a clean state
Run smoke tests against those images
Rebuild from the same commit and verify package/image/tool versions match
done_when: Two independent builds from the same commit produce matching dependency and image versions
A release checklist includes this verification

preferred_repo_changes_next: Update Dockerfile to install from the locked Python dependency file
Update frontend-angular/Dockerfile to use npm ci
Pin opencode-ai to an exact version
Replace floating image tags in docker-compose.base.yml

must_be_true_before_first_release: No floating production dependency installs remain
No unpinned global CLI installs remain
Frontend and backend builds are reproducible from a clean checkout
